questions.q: Which renewable energy source has the highest energy density?
questions.explanation: Geothermal energy provides consistent, high-density power from Earth's internal heat with capacity factors typically 70-90%, superior to solar (~15-25%) and wind (~35%) which are intermittent and require storage or backup.

questions.q: What is the primary mechanism by which solar photovoltaic cells generate electricity?
options: Converting heat from sunlight into electrical energy
Using the photoelectric effect where photons excite electrons in semiconductor materials
Burning solar radiation to produce steam
Reflecting light to concentrate heat for a generator
questions.explanation: Solar PV cells use the photoelectric effect where photons knock electrons loose from semiconductor materials (usually silicon), creating an electric current. This is fundamentally different from concentrating solar thermal systems that use heat to generate steam.

questions.q: What limits the widespread adoption of wind energy in many regions?
options: Wind turbines cannot generate electricity at night
Wind is unpredictable and intermittent, requiring energy storage or grid balancing
Solar energy is always more efficient than wind
Wind turbines are only effective at equatorial latitudes
questions.explanation: Wind's intermittency is a technical challenge; wind farms generate power only when wind speeds are within operational ranges. Modern solutions include battery storage, grid interconnection, and weather forecasting to manage this variability.

questions.q: Which renewable energy source relies on water cycle phase changes?
questions.explanation: Hydroelectric power harnesses the gravitational potential energy of water as it flows downhill through turbines, driven by precipitation and the water cycle. It's one of the oldest and most established renewable sources.

questions.q: What is the primary advantage of geothermal energy in volcanic regions?
options: It produces no greenhouse gases and has very high capacity factor and reliability
It is cheaper than all other energy sources globally
It can be deployed instantly without infrastructure
questions.explanation: Geothermal energy in tectonically active regions provides baseload power with minimal emissions and capacity factors exceeding 70%. However, it's geographically limited, still requires water for cooling, and has high upfront development costs.

questions.q: What is energy storage primarily used for in renewable energy systems?
options: To increase the energy content of renewable sources
To bridge gaps between renewable supply and electricity demand
To reduce the cost of renewable energy installation
To replace the need for fossil fuel power plants entirely
questions.explanation: Storage systems (batteries, pumped hydro, compressed air) store excess renewable energy when supply exceeds demand and release it when demand exceeds supply, balancing grid stability and reducing curtailment of renewable generation.

questions.q: Which renewable energy source has the lowest environmental impact during operation?
options: Wind turbines (which may affect bird populations)
Solar farms (with minimal emissions during operation)
questions.explanation: Solar and wind have minimal operational impacts once installed, though both have trade-offs in land use and manufacturing emissions. Hydroelectric dams significantly alter river ecosystems; biomass produces air pollution; all renewables are cleaner than fossil fuels.

questions.q: What is the theoretical maximum efficiency of a solar photovoltaic cell called?
questions.explanation: The Shockley-Queisser limit (~34% for silicon under standard sunlight) represents the maximum theoretical efficiency of a single-junction photovoltaic cell due to thermodynamic constraints. Commercial cells typically achieve 15-22% efficiency.

questions.q: Which type of renewable energy has the smallest land footprint per unit of energy produced?
questions.explanation: Wind farms have the smallest land footprint because wind turbines occupy limited ground area while allowing agricultural use beneath and around them. Solar farms require significant unshaded land; hydroelectric requires large reservoir areas; biomass requires extensive agricultural land.

questions.q: What percentage of global electricity generation currently comes from renewable sources?
questions.explanation: As of the mid-2020s, renewable energy sources (hydro, wind, solar, geothermal, biomass) provide approximately 25-30% of global electricity generation, with hydropower being the largest contributor historically, and wind/solar growing rapidly.
